        #pragma once

        #include <optional>
        #include <sqlgen.hpp>
        #include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
        #include <vector>

        namespace query_course_enrollment_stats {

        struct Course {
          sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> course_id;
          std::string title;
        };

        struct Enrollment {
          sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> enrollment_id;
          sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Course, "course_id"> course_id;
          std::optional<float> grade;
        };

        struct CourseStats {
          std::string title;
          int student_count;
          std::optional<double> avg_grade;
        };

        struct CourseStatsView {
          std::string title;
          int student_count;
          std::optional<double> avg_grade;
        };

        /**
         * Function: Calculate enrollment statistics per course.
         * 
         * @return List of course statistics
         */
        std::vector<CourseStats> query_course_enrollment_stats() {
          using namespace sqlgen::literals;

          // Connect to the database
          auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
          if (!conn_result) {
            return {};
          }
          auto conn = *conn_result;

          // Create tables if not exists
          auto create_course = sqlgen::create_table<Course> | sqlgen::if_not_exists;
          auto create_enrollment = sqlgen::create_table<Enrollment> | sqlgen::if_not_exists;

          auto course_result = create_course(conn);
          if (!course_result) {
            return {};
          }

          auto enrollment_result = create_enrollment(conn);
          if (!enrollment_result) {
            return {};
          }

          // Build the query
          auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Course, "c">(
              "title"_t1, 
              sqlgen::count("enrollment_id"_t2).as<"student_count">(), 
              sqlgen::avg("grade"_t2).as<"avg_grade">()
          )
          | sqlgen::left_join<Enrollment, "e">("course_id"_t1 == "course_id"_t2)
          | sqlgen::group_by("course_id"_t1, "title"_t1)
          | sqlgen::to<std::vector<CourseStatsView>>;

          // Execute the query
          auto result = query(conn);
          if (!result) {
            return {};
          }

          // Convert the result to vector of CourseStats
          std::vector<CourseStats> stats;
          stats.reserve(result->size());
          for (const auto& view : *result) {
            stats.push_back(CourseStats{view.title, view.student_count, view.avg_grade});
          }

          return stats;
        }

        }  // namespace query_course_enrollment_stats

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/literals.hpp>  // Added for the _c literal

namespace schema_manage_indexes {

  struct Product {
    sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
    std::string sku;
    std::string name;
    std::string category;
    double price;
  };

  bool manage_indexes() {
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;

    // Connect to the database
    auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    if (!conn_result) {
      return false;
    }
    auto conn = *conn_result;

    // Create the Product table with if_not_exists
    auto create_table_result = (sqlgen::create_table<Product> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)(conn);
    if (!create_table_result) {
      return false;
    }

    // Create the standard index on category
    auto idx_category = sqlgen::create_index<"idx_product_category", Product>("category"_c);
    idx_category.if_not_exists_ = true;
    auto idx_category_result = idx_category(conn);
    if (!idx_category_result) {
      return false;
    }

    // Create the unique index on sku
    auto idx_sku = sqlgen::create_index<"idx_product_sku", Product>("sku"_c);
    idx_sku.unique_ = true;
    idx_sku.if_not_exists_ = true;
    auto idx_sku_result = idx_sku(conn);
    if (!idx_sku_result) {
      return false;
    }

    // Create the composite index on name and price
    auto idx_name_price = sqlgen::create_index<"idx_product_name_price", Product>("name"_c, "price"_c);
    idx_name_price.if_not_exists_ = true;
    auto idx_name_price_result = idx_name_price(conn);
    if (!idx_name_price_result) {
      return false;
    }

    return true;
  }

}  // namespace schema_manage_indexes
```

     return false;

  ```cpp
  #pragma once

  #include <sqlgen.hpp>
  #include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
  #include <vector>

  namespace analyze_temporal_data {

  struct Event {
    sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> event_id;
    std::string event_name;
    sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> start_time;
    std::optional<sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S">> end_time;
    sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
  };

  struct TemporalAnalysis {
    int event_id;
    std::string event_name;
    int start_day;
    int start_month;
    int start_year;
    int start_hour;
    int start_minute;
    int start_second;
    int start_weekday;
    int64_t start_epoch;
    std::optional<double> duration_days;
  };

  std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analyze_temporal_data (const std::vector<Event>& events)  {
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;

    // Connect to the database
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

    // Write the events to the database (this creates the table and inserts the data)
    auto write_op = sqlgen::write(events);
    write_op(conn);

    // Build the query
    auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Event>(
        "event_id"_c,
        "event_name"_c,
        sqlgen::day("start_time"_c).as<"start_day">(),
        sqlgen::month("start_time"_c).as<"start_month">(),
        sqlgen::year("start_time"_c).as<"start_year">(),
        sqlgen::hour("start_time"_c).as<"start_hour">(),
        sqlgen::minute("start_time"_c).as<"start_minute">(),
        sqlgen::second("start_time"_c).as<"start_second">(),
        sqlgen::weekday("start_time"_c).as<"start_weekday">(),
        sqlgen::unixepoch("start_time"_c).as<"start_epoch">(),
        (sqlgen::unixepoch("end_time"_c) - sqlgen::unixepoch("start_time"_c)) / 86400.0 as<"duration_days">()
      )
      .where("end_time"_c.is_not_null());

    // Execute the query and convert to vector of TemporalAnalysis
    return query(conn).to<std::vector<TemporalAnalysis>>();
  }

  }  // namespace analyze_temporal_data
  ```

   namespace analyze_social_network {

   struct User {
     sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
     std::string username;
   };

   struct Friendship {
     int user1_id;
     int user2_id;
     sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
   };

   struct FriendConnection {
     std::string user1_name;
     std::string user2_name;
   };

   std::vector<FriendConnection> analyze_social_network_impl(const std::vector<User>& users, const std::vector<Friendship>& friendships) {
     // Connect to the database
     auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

     // Create tables
     sqlgen::create_table<User>(conn);
     sqlgen::create_table<Friendship>(conn);

     // Insert data
     sqlgen::write(conn, users);
     sqlgen::write(conn, friendships);

     // Read data back
     auto users_read = sqlgen::read<std::vector<User>>(conn).value();
     auto friendships_read = sqlgen::read<std::vector<Friendship>>(conn).value();

     // Build adjacency list
     std::unordered_map<int, std::unordered_set<int>> adj;
     for (const auto& f : friendships_read) {
       adj[f.user1_id].insert(f.user2_id);
       adj[f.user2_id].insert(f.user1_id);
     }

     // Build username map
     std::unordered_map<int, std::string> username_map;
     for (const auto& u : users_read) {
       username_map[u.user_id] = u.username;
     }

     // Find mutual friends
     std::vector<FriendConnection> result;
     for (int u1 : users_read) {
       for (int f1 : adj[u1]) {
         for (int f2 : adj[f1]) {
           if (f2 > u1 && adj[u1].count(f2)) {
             result.push_back(FriendConnection{username_map[u1], username_map[f2]});
           }
         }
       }
     }

     return result;
   }

   }  // namespace analyze_social_network

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>

namespace trim_and_length_validation {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct ValidatedProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string trimmed_name;
  std::string trimmed_email;
  int name_length;
  int email_length;
};

std::vector<ValidatedProfile> trim_and_length_validation() {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Read the entire UserProfile table
  auto profiles = sqlgen::read<std::vector<UserProfile>>(conn);

  // Post-process each profile
  std::vector<ValidatedProfile> result;
  for (const auto& profile : profiles) {
    // Trim the name and email
    std::string trimmed_name = profile.name;
    trimmed_name.erase(trimmed_name.begin(), std::find_if(trimmed_name.begin(), trimmed_name.end(), [](char ch) { return !std::isspace(ch); }));
    trimmed_name.erase(std::find_if(trimmed_name.rbegin(), trimmed_name.rend(), [](char ch) { return !std::isspace(ch); }).base(), trimmed_name.end());

    std::string trimmed_email = profile.email;
    trimmed_email.erase(trimmed_email.begin(), std::find_if(trimmed_email.begin(), trimmed_email.end(), [](char ch) { return !std::isspace(ch); }));
    trimmed_email.erase(std::find_if(trimmed_email.rbegin(), trimmed_email.rend(), [](char ch) { return !std::isspace(ch); }).base(), trimmed_email.end());

    // Compute the lengths
    int name_length = trimmed_name.length();
    int email_length = trimmed_email.length();

    // Create the validated profile
    ValidatedProfile validated_profile;
    validated_profile.user_id = profile.user_id;
    validated_profile.trimmed_name = trimmed_name;
    validated_profile.trimmed_email = trimmed_email;
    validated_profile.name_length = name_length;
    validated_profile.email_length = email_length;

    result.push_back(validated_profile);
  }

  return result;
}

}  // namespace trim_and_length_validation
```

 Code:

   #include <sqlgen.hpp>
   #include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
   #include <vector>
   #include <optional>

   namespace calculate_order_metrics {

   struct OrderItem {
     sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
     int order_id;
     int product_id;
     double unit_price;
     int quantity;
     std::optional<double> discount_rate;
     std::optional<double> tax_rate;
   };

   struct OrderMetrics {
     int item_id;
     int order_id;
     int product_id;
     double unit_price;
     int quantity;
     double discount_rate;
     double tax_rate;
     double gross_total;
     double discounted_total;
     double final_total;
     double unit_cost;
     int quantity_group;
     double estimated_profit;
   };

   std::vector<OrderMetrics> calculate_order_metrics_impl(const std::vector<OrderItem>& items) {
     using namespace sqlgen::literals;

     auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

     // Create the table
     conn->execute(R"(
       CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS OrderItem (
         item_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
         order_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
         product_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
         unit_price REAL NOT NULL,
         quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
         discount_rate REAL,
         tax_rate REAL
       )
     )").value();

     // Write the items to the table
     sqlgen::write(conn, items);

     // Read the data with calculated fields
     auto result = (sqlgen::read<std::vector<OrderMetrics>> 
          | sqlgen::select_from<OrderItem>(
              "item_id"_c, "order_id"_c, "product_id"_c, "unit_price"_c, "quantity"_c, 
              "discount_rate"_c, "tax_rate"_c,
              ("unit_price"_c * "quantity"_c).as<"gross_total">(),
              (("unit_price"_c * "quantity"_c) * (1 - "discount_rate"_c / 100)).as<"discounted_total">(),
              (("unit_price"_c * "quantity"_c) * (1 - "discount_rate"_c / 100) * (1 + "tax_rate"_c / 100)).as<"final_total">(),
              (("unit_price"_c * "quantity"_c) / "quantity"_c).as<"unit_cost">(),
              ("quantity"_c % 10).as<"quantity_group">(),
              ("unit_price"_c * 0.3).as<"estimated_profit">()
            )
          )(conn);

     return result.value();
   }

   }  // namespace calculate_order_metrics

   - `sqlgen::concat` to concatenate the columns and a string literal.

   auto concat(const Ts&... _ts) {
     static_assert(sizeof...(_ts) > 0,

     using Type = rfl::Tuple<typename transpilation::ToTranspilationType<

     return transpilation::Operation<transpilation::Operator::concat, Type>{
